name: forge-indian-folk-art-style-registry
description: Registry of Indian folk-art traditions supported by Forge. Each tradition has its own folder under brand/styles/<slug>/ containing style.json (machine config) and protocol.md (cultural protocol, human-editable, expert-reviewable). The registry tracks production status separately from documentation status — a tradition can be DOCUMENTED (style.json + protocol.md written) before being SHIPPED (LoRA trained, rubric authored, references collected).
_production_target_note: Target 4 traditions at production rigor by end-of-6-weeks: Madhubani (existing), Warli, Gond, Kalighat. Other 6 ship as scaffolded documentation only.

warli._special_strength: The icon king — geometric primitives survive 64×64 better than any other tradition. SVG export via potrace is first-class.

gond.named_lineage_acknowledgment: Jangarh Singh Shyam (1962-2001), Bhajju Shyam (Padma Shri 2018), Nankusia Shyam, Durgabai Vyam, Subhash Vyam, Venkat Raman Singh Shyam
gond._special_strength: Rich interior-pattern vocabulary scales beautifully to large posters. Subject-extension precedent set by Bhajju Shyam's 'The London Jungle Book' (2004).

kalighat._target: Production rigor by week 3. Bold-figure aesthetic excellent for stickers and protest-poster style.

pattachitra._target: Production rigor by week 4. Narrative-scroll aesthetic; A1/A2 poster strength.

tanjore.community_ownership: Court-folk hybrid tradition; Raju and Naidu communities historically
tanjore._caveat: Gold-foil and gesso-relief effects don't fully translate to 2D FLUX render — Tanjore will ship as 'Tanjore-inspired flat-render' with honest disclosure, not as a recreation of the physical technique.

phad.community_ownership: Joshi family lineage (primary holders); Bhopa-Bhopi narrative performers
phad._target: Scaffold by week 6. Wide-aspect scroll format — only tradition that natively fits horizontal poster ratios.

mysore._caveat: Similar gold-leaf challenge as Tanjore; ships with the same flat-render disclosure.

deferred_to_indian_miniature_family._note: These are court / academic traditions, NOT folk art. They belong in a separate engine family at brand/styles/_indian_miniature/ to be created later. Listed here for cross-reference only.

phase_2_indian_folk_traditions._note: Less internationally recognized but real folk traditions for Phase 2 expansion (month 7-8).

expansion_to_non_indian_traditions._note: Out of scope for this 6-week buildout. The system architecture (style registry + protocol registry + receipt schema) is DESIGNED to support cross-cultural expansion, but the user has scoped this phase to Indian-only. Future expansion candidates documented for architectural reference only.
expansion_to_non_indian_traditions._when: Month 10-12 or beyond, with full cultural consultation per tradition.
candidate_traditions: polish-wycinanki (largely cultural-heritage scope, lower restriction risk)

_changelog.0.1.0: Initial style registry. Madhubani (existing), Warli (new scaffold), Gond (new scaffold) ship as documented. 7 other traditions logged as planned. Cross-references the parks catalog at brand/parks/_index.json and the receipt schema at docs/SCHEMA.md.
